channel.title: NYT > U.S. > Politics
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: A Trump Obsession That Carries a Cost for Democracy
item.description: In demanding steps to address the integrity of voting, President Trump persisted in relitigating his 2020 election defeat while finding ways to cast doubt on the 2026 outcome.
item.dc:creator: Peter Baker
item.media:description: President Trump seen on a screen inside the White House briefing room as he addressed the nation on Thursday night.

item.title: Trump Released Documents on China and the 2020 Election. Here’s What We Know.
item.description: President Trump claimed that China had tried to acquire American voter data. Possessing such information would not allow votes to be manipulated.
item.dc:creator: Dustin Volz and Julian E. Barnes
item.media:description: President Trump and President Xi Jinping of China during a bilateral meeting at the G20 Summit in Japan in 2019.

item.title: Trump again makes unverifiable claims of noncitizen voting
item.description: The president offered no concrete evidence, and at least one state — Nevada — rejected his claims immediately.
item.dc:creator: Nick Corasaniti and Alexandra Berzon
item.media:credit: Bridget Bennett for The New York Times
item.media:description: President Trump claimed without evidence on Thursday that massive numbers of noncitizens are registered in four states: California, New Jersey, Nevada and Pennsylvania.

item.title: Assessing the Documents: Fake IDs From China
item.description: A previously released F.B.I. memo, viewed skeptically by intelligence agents, described a purported scheme by China to meddle in the 2020 election.
item.dc:creator: Devlin Barrett
item.media:description: The memo and other F.B.I. documents about it were released last year by Senator Charles E. Grassley, Republican of Iowa and the chairman of the Judiciary Committee.

item.title: Trump Has Dismantled Election Security Efforts. Here’s How.
item.description: Since his return to the White House, the lead federal partner for states on election security has lost around a third of its work force.
item.dc:creator: Dustin Volz
item.category: Office of the Director of National Intelligence
item.media:credit: Pool photo by Jim Lo Scalzo
item.media:description: President Trump has directed prosecutors to scrutinize a top cybersecurity aide during his first term, Christopher Krebs, who fell from his favor by validating the integrity of the 2020 election.

item.title: What to Know About Trump’s Attacks on Mail Voting
item.description: Many of President Trump’s attempts to curtail mail voting have been blocked by the courts. Before Trump, Republicans actually embraced absentee voting.
item.dc:creator: Adam Sella
item.media:credit: Patrick T. Fallon/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: President Trump continues to target voting by mail with false accusations of fraud even after court orders halted his attempts to curtail the practice before November’s midterm elections.

item.title: 5 Takeaways From a Maine Debate That Showed Replacing Platner Isn’t Easy
item.description: Maine Democrats vying to become the state’s new Senate nominee met, and mostly agreed, at a debate filled with halting answers and stilted deliveries.
item.dc:creator: Benjamin Oreskes and Tim Balk
item.media:credit: Ryan David Brown for The New York Times
item.media:description: The first hour of the debate featured four of the top Democratic candidates for Senate in Maine: from left, Shenna Bellows, Troy Jackson, Dr. Nirav Shah and Jordan Wood.

item.title: Assessing the documents: Venezuela and Smartmatic
item.dc:creator: Alan Feuer
item.media:credit: Bob Andres/Atlanta Journal-Constitution, via Associated Press
item.media:description: A Smartmatic representative demonstrating his company's system in Grovetown, Ga., in 2018.

item.title: Assessing the Documents: Voter Registration in Michigan
item.description: A well-known allegation of attempts to fraudulently register voters never yielded evidence of illegal voting.
item.dc:creator: Nick Corasaniti
item.media:description: A Michigan voter dropping their absentee ballot in the drop box in Troy, Michigan, in 2020.

item.title: Multiple Investigations Refuted Trump’s Claims That Fraud Altered the Outcome in 2020
item.description: Dozens of investigations, audits, recounts and court proceedings examined the 2020 election. None found the widespread voter fraud that President Trump claimed tilted the vote.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater
item.media:description: Ballots cast in Maricopa County, Ariz., in the 2020 general election were examined and recounted in Phoenix in May 2021. The review in Arizona found even more votes for Joseph R. Biden Jr. and fewer for President Trump than the original count.

item.title: Here’s the latest.
item.dc:creator: Maggie Haberman

item.title: Trump Administration Restricts Green Cards for Immigrants on Public Assistance
item.description: The move is a significant change that could deter hundreds of thousands of immigrants from using such programs to meet basic needs.
item.dc:creator: Madeleine Ngo and Karoun Demirjian
item.media:description: People lining up last year outside the Los Angeles building that houses offices for Immigration and Customs Enforcement and U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services.

item.title: Judge Unseals Filings From Jack Smith Subpoena for Lawmaker’s Phone Data
item.description: The Justice Department had balked at the disclosure, saying that the government had not acknowledged the special counsel’s subpoenas and that grand jury information was secret.
item.dc:creator: Charlie Savage
item.media:description: The use of subpoenas by the special counsel Jack Smith to obtain lawmakers’ call records has been known since October.

item.title: Republican Rifts Deepen Over $95 Billion Budget Plan for Iran War and SAVE Act
item.description: A House panel approved the measure. But Republicans are divided over its size, cost and policy provisions, and many are concerned about the timing before the midterm elections.
item.dc:creator: Catie Edmondson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Senator John Thune of South Dakota, the majority leader, openly questioned whether “the juice” was “worth the squeeze,” given the deep divisions in his conference over the measure.

item.title: Blue Angels’ Close Beach Flyby in Florida Results in No Punishments for Pilots
item.description: The elite flying team had announced a safety review after videos posted on social media showed a fighter jet passing just above beachgoers on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Alena Cerro
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Blue Angels pilots, in formation on July 4 in Washington, fly in F/A-18 Super Hornets.

item.title: In Prime Time, Trump Criticizes Networks for Not Carrying His Speech
item.description: The president also called for ABC and NBC to lose their broadcast licenses, after complaining that the networks were not airing his remarks.
item.dc:creator: Michael M. Grynbaum
item.media:description: President Trump was set to address the nation at 9 p.m. on Thursday night.

item.title: Tourist Helicopter Struck Geese Before Crashing in Hudson River, Documents Show
item.description: Officials discovered remains from at least two birds on the helicopter’s rotor blades, according to documents released by the National Transportation Safety Board.
item.dc:creator: Karoun Demirjian
item.media:credit: Adam Gray for The New York Times
item.media:description: City police divers searching for helicopter wreckage in the Hudson River in April 2025.

item.title: On the War Against Iran, the Pentagon Has Said Little
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has spoken about a range of issues in recent days — from testosterone to leak investigations — but he has said little about the war.
item.dc:creator: Helene Cooper
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth met with the D.C. Safe and Beautiful Task Force and the National Guard earlier this month.

item.title: How Todd Blanche Made the Justice Dept. Less Independent
item.description: Our Justice Department reporter Devlin Barrett describes how Todd Blanche, President Trump’s former personal lawyer and current nominee for attorney general, has helped Mr. Trump gain more control over the Justice Department.
item.dc:creator: Devlin Barrett, Paul Abowd, Nikolay Nikolov, Rafaela Balster, Nour Idriss and Whitney Shefte

item.title: Warnock and Ossoff, Georgia’s Senators, Ridicule Trump’s Election Fraud Claims
item.description: Senators in both parties say the state’s 2020 Senate election is a long-settled issue.
item.dc:creator: Carl Hulse
item.media:description: Senators Raphael Warnock and Jon Ossoff at a campaign event last year.

item.title: White House Teleprompter Operator Bet on Trump Speeches, Kalshi Says
item.description: A technical assistant to President Trump won around $100,000, according to the prediction market, which flagged the activity to the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Lauren McCarthy
item.media:description: Gabriel Perez, a technical assistant to President Trump, before a speech in Miami Beach last year. Kalshi said Mr. Perez had placed wagers on common words that would appear in the president’s speeches.

item.title: Republican Senator Thom Tillis Demands Todd Blanche Meet With Epstein Survivors
item.description: Senator Thom Tillis of North Carolina, a key vote on confirmation, had demanded the acting attorney general meet with victims.
item.dc:creator: Devlin Barrett
item.media:description: Todd Blanche, the acting attorney general, during his Senate Judiciary Committee hearing on Wednesday.

item.title: Could a 50-Year-Old Case Thwart Trump’s Attacks on the News Media?
item.description: The precedent said that “official harassment of the press” is forbidden by the First Amendment.
item.dc:creator: Adam Liptak
item.media:description: The Justice Department in Washington.

item.title: Trump Revisited Election Fraud Claims in Speech: What to Know.
item.description: Repeated investigations and audits have debunked claims by the president and his allies of widespread fraud or manipulation in 2020 or other elections.
item.dc:creator: Dustin Volz, Alan Feuer and Devlin Barrett
item.category: Georgia Criminal Case Against Trump (2020 Election)
item.media:credit: Rachel Mummey for The New York Times
item.media:description: People voting during the primary election at Scotch Ridge Church in Carlise, Iowa, last month.

item.title: Republican Candidates Are Hurting for Cash, but Help May Be on the Way
item.description: Super PACs and billionaires seem to be on the side of the G.O.P., new filings show.
item.dc:creator: Theodore Schleifer
item.media:credit: Antranik Tavitian for The New York Times
item.media:description: Supporters of Ken Paxton last month at the 2026 Republican Party of Texas convention in Houston. Mr. Paxton, the party’s nominee for Senate, has been drastically out-raised by his Democratic rival, James Talarico.

item.title: Democratic Senate Candidates Trounce Republicans in Fund-Raising
item.description: In the first half of this year, Democrats raised more cash in every key Senate race except Iowa.
item.dc:creator: Matt Zdun and Katherine Perry

item.title: White House Faces Stiff Pushback on Subjecting Grants to Political Review
item.description: Academics, city leaders and congressional lawmakers number among the thousands to urge the Trump administration to reconsider a plan to assert more control over grants.
item.dc:creator: Tony Romm
item.media:description: Russell T. Vought, the director of the Office of Management and Budget, testifying at a hearing in April.

item.title: How Nike’s Diversity Efforts Made It a Trump Target
item.description: A civil-rights investigation, which grew out of a 26-page memo by a political appointee, provides a road map for the White House’s assault on D.E.I.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Davis O’Brien and Kim Bhasin
item.media:description: A Nike Well Collective store in Glendale, Calif. Few major companies had more openly embraced a commitment to diversity than Nike, not only in its advertising campaigns but in its work force.

item.title: Can a Pro-Israel Democrat Still Win a Big Primary? She’s Going to Try.
item.description: To prevail in Michigan’s crucial Senate primary, Representative Haley Stevens will need to overcome Democratic voters’ skepticism of Israel. Pro-Israel groups are spending heavily to help her.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Emily Elconin for The New York Times
item.media:description: Representative Haley Stevens rarely talks about Israel on the campaign trail, preferring to focus on the Michigan economy and manufacturing, but she has made her support for the country clear.

item.title: Design Panel Signals Support for Fencing Off Park Near the White House
item.description: The Commission of Fine Arts considered the Trump administration’s proposal regarding Lafayette Square, which has been the site of numerous protests.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater
item.media:credit: Kent Nishimura for The New York Times
item.media:description: A pro-Palestinian demonstration at Lafayette Square Park in 2023. President Trump and the Secret Service have cited security concerns about such protests.

item.title: The Army Veteran Killed Outside His ‘Trump House’
item.description: Kerry Sheron could often be found waving and nodding to those who passed his home, which stood out in Escondido, Calif.
item.dc:creator: Corina Knoll
item.media:credit: Mark Abramson for The New York Times
item.media:description: A photograph of Kerry Sheron and his wife, Maria Garcia, hanging on the fence outside their home.
